this is a Windows machine and you (Claude) is running under WSL. Navigate to https://github.com and take a screenshot

> 2/11/2026, 5:29:39 PM

### Assistant
> 2/11/2026, 5:29:40 PM

[
  {
    "type": "thinking",
    "thinking": "The user wants me to navigate to GitHub in the browser and take a screenshot. Let me use the Chrome DevTools MCP tools to do this.",

### Assistant
> 2/11/2026, 5:29:40 PM

Let me navigate to GitHub and take a screenshot.

---

### Assistant
> 2/11/2026, 5:29:41 PM

[
  {
    "type": "tool_use",
    "id": "toolu_01NWFVudEwQa9fu2N3WibH6i",
    "name": "mcp__chrome-devtools__navigate_page",
    "input": {
      "type": "url",
      "url": "https://github.com"
    }
  }
]

---

> 2/11/2026, 5:29:50 PM

[
  {
    "type": "tool_result",
    "content": "Could not find Google Chrome executable for channel 'stable' at:\n - /opt/google/chrome/chrome.",
    "is_error": true,
    "tool_use_id": "toolu_01NWFVudEwQa9fu2N3WibH6i"

]

---

### Assistant
> 2/11/2026, 5:29:54 PM

[
  {
    "type": "thinking",
    "thinking": "The Chrome DevTools MCP server can't find Chrome because this is running under WSL and Chrome is installed on the Windows side. Let me try to find the Chrome executable on the Windows side.",
    "signature": ""
  }
]

---

### Assistant
> 2/11/2026, 5:29:54 PM

Chrome isn't found at the default Linux path since this is WSL. Let me check where Chrome is on the Windows side.

---

### Assistant
> 2/11/2026, 5:29:56 PM

[
  {
    "type": "tool_use",
    "id": "toolu_01VUSxbJar7DwDHSETFnJS6G",
    "name": "Bash",
    "input": {
      "command": "ls \"/mnt/c/Program Files/Google/Chrome/Application/chrome.exe\" 2>/dev/null || ls \"/mnt/c/Program Files (x86)/Google/Chrome/Application/chrome.exe\" 2>/dev/null || which google-chrome 2>/dev/null || echo \"Chrome not found\"",
      "description": "Check for Chrome executable in common Windows/WSL paths"
    }
  }
]

---

[
  {
    "tool_use_id": "toolu_01VUSxbJar7DwDHSETFnJS6G",

### Assistant
> 2/11/2026, 5:30:26 PM

[
  {
    "type": "thinking",
    "thinking": "The MCP server is trying to launch Chrome from a Linux path. The issue is that the Chrome DevTools MCP server needs Chrome to be running with remote debugging enabled. Since Chrome is on Windows, I need to launch it from the Windows side with the `--remote-debugging-port` flag, and then the MCP server should be able to connect to it.\n\nLet me try launching Chrome from WSL using the Windows executable with remote debugging enabled.",
    "signature": ""
  }
]

---

### Assistant
> 2/11/2026, 5:30:27 PM

The Chrome DevTools MCP server can't find Chrome at the Linux path. Let me try launching Chrome from the Windows side with remote debugging enabled so the MCP server can connect.

---

### Assistant
> 2/11/2026, 5:30:28 PM

[
  {
    "type": "tool_use",
    "id": "toolu_01FDStrHU2stWqZpPnXuBrgU",
    "name": "Bash",
    "input": {
      "command": "\"/mnt/c/Program Files/Google/Chrome/Application/chrome.exe\" --remote-debugging-port=9222 \"https://github.com\" &>/dev/null &\necho \"Chrome launched with remote debugging on port 9222\"\nsleep 3",
      "description": "Launch Chrome on Windows side with remote debugging port"
    }
  }
]

---
